sections.heading: Sector 1
sections.body:
As you launch from the start finish line, keep as far to the right as you can through the kink just before turn one. The first part of sector one is one of the trickiest to get right, especially in race trim, so make sure you are leaving yourself some headroom for lockups and mistakes if you are learning this circuit for the first time.

As you enter the braking zone of turn 1, braking at the orange marked section of wall, try to turn in so that the left side of your car mounts the kerb and sets you up in as straight of a line as possible to be able to hit turn two in the best shape.

Hug the inside kerb before moving out wide on the exit of turn 2.

As you reach the apex of turn 2, shift down from fourth to third and hug the right-hand side kerb. This is a hotspot for lighting up your rear tyres and finding yourself facing the wrong way after what will feel like a very silly, slow-speed spin. Just make sure you arent too generous with that right foot as you accelerate away from the inside kerb and head out towards the left-hand side wall at the exit of turn 2.

This trio of corners will see you through the rest of sector one. Turn three is a hot spot for lap one crashes, so make sure you use extra caution here when other cars are around you. Brake at the fifty-meter board and be aggressive with your turn in. F1 25 is similar to last years title in that there is a lot of grip on offer, so really utilise those Pirellis and get your car across the inside kerb. Get it ready to go back the other way for the second half of the chicane, touching the inside apex and running the car as wide on exit as you dare, being careful not to brush the wall.

Turn 5 in Canada is flat out, just millimetres from the wall!

The final corner of sector one is easily flat out in these modern Formula One cars. There is a small apex on the left just before the turn-in. Hit that, and then your car will be lined up nicely for the inside line all the way around the long turn five. On the exit, follow the darker tarmac and drift ot the middle of the circuit to carry as much speed as possible before bringing it back to the right-hand side kerbs, ready for the entry to turn six and the start of the second sector!

sections.heading: Sector 2
sections.body:
Another overtaking hotspot at this circuit is turns six and seven. If you had a bad exit or had to slow down, be aware that other cars may well be coming from behind at full speed and eyeing up a move down the inside into this slower chicane.

As you enter the braking zone for turn six, spot the one-hundred meter board and brake just before the nose of your car passes it, once you spot the light grey tarmac on the outside of the track, start to decrease your brake pressure and begin the turning process, smoothly and with extra care of other cars as previosuly mentioned.

The apex is right in the middle of the deep red kerb. Be careful not to take too much of it; track limits can ruin your lap here in an instant. Straight up your car as quickly as you can and make sure you plant your right foot all the way to the floor, catapulting you through turn seven and into your first of two DRS zones.

Be careful of the high kerb on the right!

The fastest chicane on the track, turns eight and nine, are a rollercoaster! Your braking point will slightly differ if you have the use of DRS or not. If you are not using it, break just as you reach the lip of the tunnel, and if you are, break at the marker board just before it. Once you have dabbed the brakes, use the lighter tarmac on the left-hand side after the tunnel to widen your entry as much as possible, similar to turn six, and take a conservative amount of kerb. Too much, and that could be your race over.

Turn seven is flat out as you straighten the car up and hug the left-hand side of the circuit, making sure to drift out wide again to the right-hand side of the following straight. The DRS detection zone is on the exit of turn seven, so despite having to tackle the hairpin next, make sure you are within a second of the car in front if you want that big performance boost down the main backstraight!

sections.heading: Sector 3
sections.body:
Heading out of the fastest chicane, you will soon see a tight right-hand hairpin. Make sure you are all the way over to the left and brake hard at the 100 marker board. Slowly turn in as you row down the gears. Try to give yourself the latest apex you can without going wide, so you have the best and most direct run through the kink left and onto the back straight.

Be careful not to hit the Wall of Champions, it has ruined races for quite a few of the biggest names in the sport...

Once you reach the end of said back straight, brake hard again and drop to sixth gear for the final fast chicane of the lap, this section ends with what is known as the Wall of Champions. Use as much kerb as you dare coming out of the second section and power over the left-hand side of the start line, ready to sweep right and set yourself up for turn one again!
